properties.remark: 6 TO 8 HIGH TENSION TELEPHONE POLES BROKEN OFF AT 20 FEET. 2 FOOT TREE DOWN NEARBY.

SPOTTER CALLED IN WITH REPORTS OF NUMEROUS TREES DOWN IN AND AROUND THE VICKSBURG AREA. DAMAGE OCCURED AROUND 435 PM.
SPOTTER WITNESSED 2 6 FOOT SECTIONS OF FENCE UPROOTED AND THROWN ACROSS THE PROPERTY. ESTIMATED WIND SPEEDS TO 60 MPH.
NUMEROUS TREES DOWN IN THE VICINITY BETWEEN TOWNLEY AND FISK ROADS AND SPRINPORT ROAD.
NUMEROUS TREES AND POWER POLES DOWN IN CITY OF MARSHALL..

NUMEROUS POWER POLES DOWN. 1/2 INCH HAIL REPORTED IN LESLIE.

HAIL NOW COMING TO AN END
TREE DAMAGE ALSO OCCURED WITH BRANCHES DOWN. GAS STATION LOST ITS SIGN.

CENTRAL DISPATCH AND ROAD COMMISSION REPORT SEVERAL TREES DOWN IN YANKEE SPRINGS TOWNSHIP. THE TREES RANGE FROM 12 INCHES TO 24 INCHES IN DIAMETER. THE HEALTH IS UNKNOWN. THE TREES WERE REPORTED DOWN NEAR W M-179 AND S WHITMORE RD...W M-179 AND YANKEE SPRINGS RD...AND W M-179 AND NORRIS RD. THE TIME OF THE EVENT WAS RADAR ESTIMATED.
A LARGE TREE BETWEEN 1-2 FT DIAMETER WAS REPORTED DOWN NEAR 108TH ST AND HARRIS CREEK RD IN THORNAPPLE TOWNSHIP. THE EVENT TIME WAS RADAR ESTIMATED.
6 TO 8 HEALTHY TREES DOWN BETWEEN BLAIR AND WISNER ROADS ON JOHNSON ROAD. TREES WERE A FOOT IN DIAMETER. ALSO PINE TREE WAS UPROOTED IN THE SAME AREA.
STATE POLICE REPORT LARGE TREE FELL ON CAR NEAR DOWNTOWN SAUGATUCK.
STATE POLICE REPORT LARGE TREE DOWN BLOCKING ROAD AT 128TH AND 30TH STREETS.
1 FOOT DIAMETER TREE DOWN AT CR-681 AND 48TH.
STRONG THUNDERSTORMS WINDS BLOW DOWN A TREE IN FIELD IN WAYLAND AROUND 730 PM.
A 20 X 30 BARN WAS DESTROYED.
SEVERAL TREES DOWN NORTH AND SOUTH OF THE CITY OF ALLEGAN. SOME TREES BLOCKING ROADS.
TREES AND WIRES DOWN. TIME ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR.
WIND GUST MEASURED AT GLERL OB SITE ON SOUTH HAVEN SOUTH PIER HEAD.
POWER LINES DOWN. NUMEROUS TREES DOWN IN THE VICINITY OF COMSTOCK.
NUMEROUS TREES DOWN ACROSS NORTHERN PART OF COUNTY.
STRONG THUNDERSTORM WINDS HAVE BLOWN DOWN MULTIPLE LARGE TREE BRANCHES AND POWER LINES. POWER IS OUT IN THE TOWN OF LAWRENCE.
POWER LINES DOWN. NUMEROUS TREES DOWN IN THE VICINITY OF COMSTOCK.

STREET FLOODING WAS REPORTED AT 17TH AND OTTAWA WITH ROAD IMPASSIBLE.
CRACKING AND POPPING OF TREES HEARD. TORRENTIAL RAINS.
WATER REPORTED UP TO THE FOUNDATIONS OF HOMES NEAR 32ND AND OTTAWA.
FLOODING OF TERMINAL BUILDING AT THE GRAND RAPIDS AIRPORT. OVER 2 INCHES OF RAIN IN TWO HOURS BETWEEN 7 PM AND 9 PM.

EF2 TORNADO ON THE GROUND FOR 5.7 MILES. ONE QUARTER MILE WIDE. TOUCHED DOWN AT 940 PM. LIFTED AT 946 PM. HOUSE DESTROYED FROM NUMEROUS FALLEN TREES. PARTIAL ROOF TORN OFF HOUSE WITH ONE WALL COLLAPSED. POLE BARN COMPLETELY DESTROYED. METAL BUILDING BLOWN DOWN. LARGE BARN MOVED OFF CINDER BLOCKS.
THE RIVER WAS REPORTED OVER 12TH STREET NEAR 135TH AVE.
CARS STALLED IN FLOODWATERS ON 52ND NEAR BRETON.
EF1 TORNADO ON GROUND FOR 1.75 MILES. 300 YARDS WIDE. TOUCHED DOWN AT 953 PM. LIFTED AT 956 PM. ONE BUILDING DESTROYED. NUMEROUS TREES DESTORYED.
SEVERAL TREES DOWN. ONE LARGE TREE UPROOTED IN A BACKYARD. OTHER TREES DOWN BLOCKING ROADS. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.
WIDESPREAD FLOODING OF STREETS IN WAYLAND.

EF2 TORNADO ON THE GROUND FOR 1.25 MILES. 300 YARDS WIDE. TOUCHED DOWN AT 1008 PM. LIFTED AT 1010 PM. ENTIRE ROOF TORN OFF HOUSE. DECK ALSO COMPLETELY TORN OFF. DEBRIS THROWN 150 YARDS FROM HOUSE. INSULATION FOUND OVER ONE MILE AWAY.

WIDESPREAD FLOODING IN THE HOLLAND AREA. SEWAGE BACKING INTO BASEMENTS ON EAST SIDE OF 112TH ABOUT ONE THIRD OF A MILE SOUTH OF RILEY. MANY HOMES FLOODED. STORM TOTAL OF 6.87 INCHES OF RAIN MEASURED IN THIS AREA.

100 TREES SNAPPED OR UPROOTED AT SANDY PINES RESORT
NUMEROUS TREES DOWN IN SALEM TOWNSHIP.
NUMEROUS TREES DOWN IN SALEM TOWNSHIP.
TREES AND POWER LINES DOWN. RAINFALL 1.25
NUMEROUS LARGE TREES DOWN FROM HASTING TO THE EAST IN HASTINGS AND CASTELTON TOWNSHIPS.
TWO WATER SPOUTS HAVE BEEN REPORTED OFFSHORE NEAR LAKE MACATAWA AND WERE MOVING INLAND AT 1040AM. REPORT IS RELAYED BY OTTAWA COUNTY CENTRAL DISPATCH.

THE WATER SPOUT MOVED ONSHORE NEAR LAKE MACATAWA AND CONTINUES TO HAVE ROTATION FROM 11AM TO 1110AM. IT IS UNCLEAR WHETHER CONTACT WITH GROUND IS IN PROGRESS...BUT WITNESSES SAY IT IS RAISING AND LOWERING.
SEVERAL TREES DOWN IN THE ROAD NEAR 32ND AND LUCE.

JUST NORTH OF 56TH STREET ON WILSON AVENUE WATER WAS FLOWING OVER THE ROAD. THREE VEHICLES WERE STRANDED WITH WATER UP TO THE MIDDLE OF THE DOORS. ONE PICKUP TRUCK WAS ROTATED SIDEWAYS BY THE MOVING WATER.
*** 1 FATAL *** 35 YEAR OLD MALE WAS KILLED WHILE DRIVING ON 15000 BLOCK OF MERCURY DRIVE WHEN A 10 INCH DIAMETER OAK TREE BRANCH FELL ON HIS MOVING CAR. TWO PASSENGERS WERE NOT INJURED.
MUSKEGON ASOS PEAK WIND OBSERVATION
LARGE TREES AND BRANCHES DOWN ON 40TH AND 48TH STREETS BETWEEN WILSON AND CLEVELAND.
SHINGLES BLOWN OFF OF BARN.

KENT COUNTY DISPATCH REPORTS TREES AND WIRES DOWN WITH ACTIVE FIRES IN AND AROUND THE CITY OF SPARTA AT THE FOLLOWING LOCATIONS...11200 STUBBINS....3506 14 MILE NW...70 CARRIE IN THE TOWN OF SPARTA...53 ELMWOOD IN THE TOWN OF SPARTA...PEACH RIDGE AND 12 MILE ROAD...AND PEACH RIDGE AND 14 MILE ROAD. ALL REPORTED BETWEEN 650 AND 65 PM. LIGHTNING MAY HAVE CAUSED MANY OF THE FIRES.
2 FOOT DIAMETER TREE UPROOTED. 8 INCH DIAMETER HEALTHY TREE SNAPPED. MANY ADDITIONAL TREES UPROOTED OR BENT OVER.
ONE QUARTER INCH HAIL ALSO REPORTED.
A POWER POLE IN GREAT CONDITION WAS SNAPPED COMPLETELY OFF. POWER LINES DRAPED ACROSS THE ROAD. MANY MORE HEALTHY AND ROTTEN TREES DOWN.
14 INCH DIAMETER TREE DOWN. POWER LINES DOWN.
THUNDERSTORM WINDS TORE OFF A PORTION OF THE ROOF AT JACKSON NORTHWEST KIDDER MIDDLE SCHOOL...CAUSING AN ESTIMATED 400 THOUSAND DOLLARS IN DAMAGE. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.
NUMEROUS TREES DOWN.
A 30 INCH DIAMETER TREE AND A 15 INCH DIAMETER TREE REPORTED DOWN.
MULTIPLE TREES REPORTED DOWN AROUND CRYSTAL LAKE.
ONE TREE DOWN IN GRAND HAVEN TOWNSHIP.
TREE DOWN.

APPROXIMATELY A DOZEN POPLAR TREES SNAPPED...ALL FALLING TOWARD THE NORTHEAST. A SMALL BRANCH PIERCED A CLOSED IN PORCH. A TRAMPOLINE WAS THROWN APPROXIMATELY 25 YARDS. WINDS WERE ESTIMATED AT AROUND 65 MPH.

APPROXIMATELY SIX TREES WERE SNAPPED...AND ALL FELL TOWARD THE EAST. A FEW SHINGLES WERE BLOWN OFF A ROOF. A TRAVEL TRAILER WAS TIPPED OVER TOWARD THE EAST. A TRAMPOLINE WAS THROWN 25 YARDS. WINDS WERE ESTIMATED AROUND 70 MPH.
